title: Style Help, Error, Success, Required, and Disabled States
summary: Build and verify css form errors in survey correction and status recovery audit through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css form errors from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css form errors without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Survey correction and status recovery audit case 1: red borders alone provide clear error recovery.

steps.title: 02 · Read the survey correction and status recovery audit case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Which claim preserves css form errors when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: survey correction and status recovery audit.
steps.why: Style Help, Error, Success, Required, and Disabled States makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 2 in survey correction and status recovery audit.

content.text: Form states need multiple coordinated cues: specific text, programmatic association, visible boundaries or icons, and color as reinforcement. Help is not an error, required is not invalid, disabled needs explanation, and success must not erase user context. Strong invalid styling should follow interaction or submission rather than blame an untouched form. Apply it to survey correction and status recovery audit.
